prompt:
あなたはTOEIC Part 6のテスト作成専門家です。
長文穴埋め問題を作成してください。

TOEIC Part 6の形式：
- 1つの文書（メール、記事、広告など）に4つの空欄
- 各空欄に4つの選択肢（A, B, C, D）
- 語彙・文法・文脈理解を総合的に評価
- ビジネス文書形式

重要な要求事項：
1. 自然で実用的なビジネス文書を作成
2. 文法的に正確で、ネイティブスピーカーが自然に感じる表現
3. TOEIC Part 6の形式と難易度に準拠
4. 4つの空欄を適切な位置に配置
5. 各空欄に4つの選択肢を提供
6. 正解は1つのみ、他は明確に不正解
7. 日本語解説も含める

出力形式はJSON形式で以下の構造にしてください：
{
  "documentType": "指定された文書タイプ",
  "title": "文書のタイトル",
  "fullText": "空欄を(1)、(2)、(3)、(4)で示した完全な文書",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": ["選択肢A", "選択肢B", "選択肢C", "選択肢D"],
      "correct": "A|B|C|D",
      "explanation": "なぜその答えが正しいかの説明（日本語）",
      "questionType": "vocabulary|grammar|context|sentence_insertion"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "easy|medium|hard",
  "topic": "ビジネス分野",
  "wordCount": 文書の単語数
}

以下の条件でTOEIC Part 6問題を1問作成してください：

【生成条件】
- 難易度: hard
- 文書タイプ: article (Article/Report)
- ビジネストピック: financial_analysis (財務分析)
- 空欄数: 4個

【特別な要求】
各空欄の正解位置は以下のように配置してください：
- 空欄1の正解位置: C
- 空欄2の正解位置: C
- 空欄3の正解位置: A
- 空欄4の正解位置: A

【問題タイプの配分】
- 空欄1: vocabulary (語彙選択)
- 空欄2: context (文脈理解)
- 空欄3: grammar (文法・語形)
- 空欄4: sentence_insertion (文挿入)

JSON形式で問題を作成してください。

{
  "documentType": "article",
  "title": "Quarterly Financial Review: Q2 2025",
  "fullText": "Quarterly Financial Review: Q2 2025\n\nOur analysis of the second-quarter results for 2025 indicates mixed signals regarding the company's financial resilience. The company's liquidity ratios are indicative of its ability to meet short-term obligations; however, the operating cash flow has been persistently (1) over the past two fiscal years. This trend, coupled with an increase in accounts receivable days, raises concerns about cash conversion and short-term funding needs.\n\nAlthough top-line revenue growth was maintained at 2.8% year-over-year, margins have been under pressure. While revenue growth remained modest, the adjusted EBITDA margin improved, (2) the decline in net income attributed to foreign exchange losses and higher interest expense. We note that the margin expansion was driven primarily by cost controls and the phasing out of low-margin product lines.\n\nAn impairment loss recognized in Q1 affected comparability with prior periods. Management contends that, had the impairment been recognized earlier, the carrying values of the related intangible assets (3) their recoverable amounts. Nevertheless, the one-time charge reduced equity and tightened leverage ratios as measured by debt-to-equity and net debt-to-EBITDA.\n\n(4)\n\nLooking ahead, revenue drivers include the migration to subscription pricing for select software modules, geographic expansion into Southeast Asia, and continued emphasis on cross-selling to existing enterprise clients. The company's ability to convert these growth initiatives into sustainable free cash flow will determine its refinancing flexibility in the next twelve months.",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": [
        "voluminous",
        "erratic",
        "depressed",
        "accelerating"
      ],
      "correct": "C",
      "explanation": "正解は C: \"depressed\"。文は「営業キャッシュフローが過去2会計年度にわたって持続的に（1）している」と述べており、ここでは \"depressed\"（低迷している、押し下げられている）が意味的に適切。A \"voluminous\"（大量の）は量を示す語で文脈に合わない。B \"erratic\"（不規則な）は変動性を示すが、'persistently'（持続的に）と矛盾する。D \"accelerating\"（加速している）は意味が逆で、文脈に合わない。 (questionType: vocabulary)"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank2",
      "position": 2,
      "options": [
        "due to",
        "consequent to",
        "despite",
        "along with"
      ],
      "correct": "C",
      "explanation": "正解は C: \"despite\"。文全体は「調整後EBITDAマージンは改善したが、純利益の下落にもかかわらず（despite）」という対比を示しており、\"despite\" が適切。A \"due to\" や B \"consequent to\" は原因を示すため、\"margin improved due to the decline in net income\" のように読むと意味が不自然（因果関係が逆）。D \"along with\"（〜とともに）は併存を示すだけで、対比の意味を出せない。 (questionType: context)"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank3",
      "position": 3,
      "options": [
        "would have more accurately reflected",
        "would have more accurately reflect",
        "will have more accurately reflected",
        "would more accurately reflect"
      ],
      "correct": "A",
      "explanation": "正解は A: \"would have more accurately reflected\"。仮定法過去完了の構文 \"had the impairment been recognized earlier\" に続く主節には過去の事実と異なる仮定の結果を表す過去完了（would have + 過去分詞）が必要で、語順と形も A が正しい。B は動詞の形が誤り（\"reflect\" が原形になっており不正）、C は未来完了で時制が合わない、D は仮定法の結果として完了形（have + 過去分詞）を欠いており意味がずれる。 (questionType: grammar)"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank4",
      "position": 4,
      "options": [
        "We recommend that the board prioritize deleveraging through targeted asset disposals and disciplined capital-expenditure restraints to restore balance-sheet flexibility.",
        "Management has already finalized plans to increase leverage and invest in speculative ventures.",
        "The accounting treatment for impairment does not affect the cash flows within the reporting period.",
        "Investors expect immediate dividend increases even as leverage worsens."
      ],
      "correct": "A",
      "explanation": "正解は A。前の文でワンタイムチャージにより資本が減少しレバレッジが悪化したことが述べられているため、続く文として『当面のバランスシートの柔軟性を回復するために減債（資産売却等）や資本支出抑制を優先することを取締役会に推奨する』という勧告文が自然に接続する。B は意味が逆で不適切、C は一般論かつ文脈にそぐわない（減損が資金流出に影響しないとは限らない）、D は話の焦点が異なり文脈上不適切。 (questionType: sentence_insertion)"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "hard",
  "topic": "financial_analysis",
  "wordCount": 228
}

あなたはTOEIC Part 6問題の翻訳専門家です。
文書全体と選択肢を適切に日本語に翻訳してください。

重要なルール：
1. 文書は自然で分かりやすい日本語に翻訳
2. 文書の翻訳では、各空欄に正解を埋めた完全な文として翻訳すること
3. 選択肢は1-4語の簡潔な日本語に翻訳
4. 選択肢に説明や解説は絶対に不要
5. 文脈に応じた最も適切な意味のみ
6. ビジネス用語は適切な日本語表現を使用
7. 4つの空欄すべてに対する選択肢翻訳を提供すること

出力形式：
{
  "titleTranslation": "タイトルの翻訳",
  "contentTranslation": "文書全体の翻訳（各空欄に正解を埋めた完全な文として翻訳）",
  "questionsTranslations": [
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    },
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    },
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    },
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    }
  ]
}

次のTOEIC Part 6問題を翻訳してください：

タイトル：Quarterly Financial Review: Q2 2025

文書全体（空欄あり）：
Quarterly Financial Review: Q2 2025

Our analysis of the second-quarter results for 2025 indicates mixed signals regarding the company's financial resilience. The company's liquidity ratios are indicative of its ability to meet short-term obligations; however, the operating cash flow has been persistently (1) over the past two fiscal years. This trend, coupled with an increase in accounts receivable days, raises concerns about cash conversion and short-term funding needs.

Although top-line revenue growth was maintained at 2.8% year-over-year, margins have been under pressure. While revenue growth remained modest, the adjusted EBITDA margin improved, (2) the decline in net income attributed to foreign exchange losses and higher interest expense. We note that the margin expansion was driven primarily by cost controls and the phasing out of low-margin product lines.

An impairment loss recognized in Q1 affected comparability with prior periods. Management contends that, had the impairment been recognized earlier, the carrying values of the related intangible assets (3) their recoverable amounts. Nevertheless, the one-time charge reduced equity and tightened leverage ratios as measured by debt-to-equity and net debt-to-EBITDA.

(4)

Looking ahead, revenue drivers include the migration to subscription pricing for select software modules, geographic expansion into Southeast Asia, and continued emphasis on cross-selling to existing enterprise clients. The company's ability to convert these growth initiatives into sustainable free cash flow will determine its refinancing flexibility in the next twelve months.

各空欄の正解：
空欄1: depressed
空欄2: despite
空欄3: would have more accurately reflected
空欄4: We recommend that the board prioritize deleveraging through targeted asset disposals and disciplined capital-expenditure restraints to restore balance-sheet flexibility.

選択肢：
空欄1: A. voluminous, B. erratic, C. depressed, D. accelerating
空欄2: A. due to, B. consequent to, C. despite, D. along with
空欄3: A. would have more accurately reflected, B. would have more accurately reflect, C. will have more accurately reflected, D. would more accurately reflect
空欄4: A. We recommend that the board prioritize deleveraging through targeted asset disposals and disciplined capital-expenditure restraints to restore balance-sheet flexibility., B. Management has already finalized plans to increase leverage and invest in speculative ventures., C. The accounting treatment for impairment does not affect the cash flows within the reporting period., D. Investors expect immediate dividend increases even as leverage worsens.

重要：
1. 文書の翻訳では、各空欄に上記の正解を埋めた完全な文として翻訳してください
2. questionsTranslationsは必ず4つの要素を含む配列で、各要素が4つの選択肢翻訳を含むこと
3. 選択肢は簡潔に翻訳してください

JSON形式で翻訳を出力してください。
